en.text: the brahmachārīs, celibate seekers of the Absolute
O Arjuna, best of the Bhāratas
O best of embodied beings, Arjuna
and of aprākṛta or supramundane form – medium-sized – of human feature
am the Lord of sacrifice in the form of the Supersoul, the sanctioner and rewarder of the (sacrificial etc.) actions of the living beings
the deities of fire and light
are knowers of day and night
with the coming of the day of Lord Brahmā
one-pointedly (without the practices of karma, jñān, etc., or without desiring their objectives of heaven, liberation, etc.)
merciful teacher of devotion unto Himself
who is known as avyakta, ‘the unmanifest.’
who is more subtle than the atom
which manifests the bodies of the human and other species of life through the agency of gross and subtle material elements
which is the abode of suffering
this My supremacy and the supremacy of My devotion
of the world (where persons are apt to follow the paths of jñān and karma)
any (bhakti-) yogī (one engaged in the devotional service of the Lord)
who or what is Adhiyajña, the agent of sacrifice
the deity of the waning moon
who constantly aspires for My divine association
the supreme goal the spiritual plane or sālokya-mukti
have been mentioned in the scriptures
is the collective universal form, in which are contained the demigods, headed by the solar gods
comprises the duration of one thousand chatur-yugas
all the gateways of the senses stopping them from accepting the objects of the senses
universal progeny with their bodies, senses, enjoyable objects, and abodes
Along with the method of attaining it
the deity of the waxing moon
and is transcendental to material nature
to this unmanifest Hiraṇyagarbha (‘born within the golden egg, the universe’), Lord Brahmā
for such a person in a relationship of dāsya, sakhya, etc
having absorbed his mind in thought of it
and the deity of the sun’s six-monthly southern ascent
and the deity of the sun’s six-monthly northern ascent
the learned knowers of the Vedas
the creative force (or offerings to a demigod)
in samādhi through the practice of dhāraṇā, yogic concentration
of the duration of one thousand chatur-yugas
from Brahmaloka, the highest plane in the universe, downwards
possessing a radiant form, which, like the sun, illuminates both Himself and others

ru.text: непреходящим (вечным) и непогрешимым (свободным от заблуждений и мирской скверны)
Брахман («нейтральная территория» между миром Всевышнего и материальным миром, мир чистого сознания)
в то время, когда она светит
единая по своей природе с Брахманом
единая, свободная от мирской скверны надмирная Реальность
